title: check-refs gate: enforce grounded-leaf statement string-match vs refs/ (no trust-me pass)
description: Add a gate (in check-defs.py or a new scripts/check-refs.py wired into check-all.sh) that, for every grounded leaf (status=grounded), recomputes the refs/ payload sha256 and grep -F's the leaf statement at the recorded locus — failing the build on mismatch. Mirrors the existing cited-def byte-check in check-defs.py. This makes the user's 'only string-matches count' rule MACHINE-ENFORCED rather than a convention. Depends on the cited-audit (status rename + locus/sha256 recorded).
notes: CORE GATE BUILT + shipped (commit e5b21c8): scripts/check-refs.py byte-matches every af-WORKSPACE external's verbatim quote vs its refs/ locus, wired into check-all.sh (pre-commit hook now BLOCKS fabricated quotes); 17 red->green tests. It already caught+blocked the GT-bhsa-jc fabrication. This bead stays open only because its full scope (REGISTRY grounded-leaf enforcement, status=grounded) ties to the cited->grounded rename in aipm-17f; gate HARDENING (skip_noquote evasion, whole-quote match, retrofit lem-P-properties' 7 quote-less externals) is aipm-iel.

The campaign's delta=0 anchor therefore lacks a byte-pinned proof source. Acquire the H-M source (book: Probability Measures on Semigroups, or the original paper) via TIB, byte-pin into refs/ with manifest+lock entries, then re-run the proof autopsy (notes/swarm-answers/w105_bsautopsy.md) against the REAL proof. Norm-convention landmine documented there (entrywise vs row-mass).

title: [open-math] sigma_v-wall lemma: the terminal residual of the HLC programme
description: After the full campaign (3 model families, 30+ delegations, 67k+ verified instances): HLC <= sigma_v-wall (notes/endgame-sigma-wall-residual.md): (A) hidden vertex with external mass sigma_v <= 1/2 has H <= B_A*sigma_v*tau; (B) sigma_v >= 1/2 and H > B_B*tau forces exposure. Measured: B_B = 0.536, floor a = 3.48. Glue to HLC: a = min(4/B_A^2, 1/B_B^2). Sub-residual: psi-gap lemma (one route only). Next: (1) rerun d8 decider PERSISTING LP dual certificates (methodology gap found by endgame worker); (2) fable closer on the sigma_v-wall with the dual certificates in hand.
notes: FINAL KERNEL (2026-06-11, post wave-13): the entire chain rests on ONE inequality — the path-product floor Pi_C >~ tau - O(L*delta) for band components of sigma-tilde > tau hidden top vertices (thin-chain exclusion). Prover DIED-AT it; two refuters NOT-CONSTRUCTED against it (incl. the periodic template attacking the finisher's primitivity gap — fails via an instructive dichotomy). Everything else proved/proved-mod-audit: component finisher (w12), s8 branch, corner theorem, t10 finisher, existential-DMF chain. Empirics: linear law delta >= H/2 fits the entire record; no instance has ever entered the open regime. Unwalked: Hognas-Mukherjea real proof (separate bead), chain-specialized SOS certificate search, honest-stall write-up (report v3 carries the structure). Full record: notes/wave5-sigma-wall-parallel.md + notes/swarm-answers/ (40+ archived verdicts).

title: [open-math] HLC: high-shell localization/cost lemma — THE single remaining open for op-exposed-hull
description: W2d grand assembly (notes/wave2/W2d-grand-assembly.md): op-exposed-hull is PROVED conditional on HLC with C'=max(4A,1/sqrt(a)). HLC: P1=1, P^2=P, neg<=delta<=delta0, tau=sqrt(delta), rho=4tau, kappa=tau/4, W=(rho,kappa)-exposed row vertices, H=max_i dist_1(p_i,conv W). If H>=A*rho and delta<a*H^2 then some row vertex at height >=H/2 is (rho,kappa)-well-exposed. ABSORBS: FTI-2 (aipm-nhj), no-staircase (aipm-mox), dual-localization, good-frame transfer, A2-stall repair. P(true)~0.7, P(provable now)~0.45 (codex calibration). Refuters at delta/H^2~280 floor; the formal shadow construction violates exact idempotence — P^2=P is the wall.
notes: State after fable pass + wave-4 audits: 7 new facts banked (F-WR wiggle rigidity confirmed w/ side conditions; F-E/F-GB constants honest-ified; F-ND c=0.85; F-BC kappa+delta), F-psi conditional (missing: uniform psi-gap lemma). Residual open = MRP two-level regime + psi-gap. P(HLC)~0.75-0.8. MRP exact-LP decider running. Key docs: notes/fable-hlc-attack.md, notes/wave4/audit-summary.md

title: [open-math] FTI-2: exact skinny-pair completion cost (the op-exposed-hull crux)
description: Sharp deciding case after D0-D6 (branch agent-a/classical-portfolio): exact P (P1=1,P^2=P,neg<=delta), v1,v2 distinct row vertices at dist>=H from conv A (A subset W), both failing (4tau,tau/4)-exposedness, mutual shadows with mu->1 — does max neg >= a*H^2? Attack via projection-norm formulation ||P||>=1+cH^2. Numerical decider specified in notes/d6-codex-frame-transfer.md. P(true)~0.85 (codex calibration).

title: af-validate thm-matrix-splitting (= obs-matrix-audit re-audit)
description: Why: the suspect critical-path piece; obs-matrix-audit/aipm-36d flag the sector-reconstruction step as recorded-as-B-claim-only, unverified (earlier Haagerup route had an ordinary-vs-cb gap). af-validating with fresh per-node verifiers IS the independent re-audit. notes: SOUNDNESS AUDIT COMPLETE (opus, 2026-06-09) -> verdict NEEDS-REFORMULATION (NOT false; do not abandon). SOUND: constant C genuinely n-independent; the matching-curvature DETECTION-lemma identity (||d1 S_mu||_inj >= 1/2 ||M_kappa||, arbitrary matrix into FULL Schur multiplier) verified to machine precision -> does NOT reintroduce the ordinary-vs-cb amplification gap; the log-Schur stress test (obs-matrix-audit's sharpest) RESOLVED: dist/||f|| ratio FLAT ~1.3 across n=8..36, NOT a counterexample (||M|| and ||f|| both grow log-like). R (const 60) and C (const 124) sectors fully explicit + hand-verified. THREE reformulation points: (1) contract overstates closure - quaternionic mixed-sector twirl P_M G_ij=P_M A_ij + quaternionic scalar amplification lemma are ASSERTED not derived (the one compressed step; expand the {1,i,j,k}-isotypic orbit arithmetic before any checkmark); (2) state C as 'some universal C', drop per-sector numerals 60/84/124 from the CONTRACT (keep in proof); (3) scope: averaging is over index-block triples in the FIXED frame, not Jordan frames (distinguish from open full-Layer-1). Buildable after (1); must FACTOR into ~8 sub-lemmas. Full verdict archived in agent transcript.

title: Resolve lem-cstar 'B(H)' vs dilation-space D (finite-dim C*) import gap in thm-dilation-compatible
description: Capstone verifier (agent-A) raised blocking challenge ch-d3306c16f56 on thm-dilation-compatible node 1.3: lem-cstar-sa-to-epsjb's contract + grounding (Kitaev th_almost_idemp :2192-2194, GT-bhsa-jc, GT-jb-orderunit-3310) are for 'UCP map on B(H)', but the parent's F=jC is UCP on the dilation space D, a general finite-dim C*-algebra (report 10-exponent.tex:80 declares D 'a finite-dimensional C*-algebra', not B(K)). Node 1.3 substitutes D into the B(H) role without discharge. Claim almost certainly TRUE (finite-dim C* embeds unitally in B(K); Choi-Effros C*-generic). FIX OPTIONS: (i) widen lem-cstar contract 'B(H)' -> 'finite-dim/unital C*-algebra' + re-ground B(H)_sa-specific externals (GT-bhsa-jc -> 'C*-algebra sa is JC') + re-validate lem-cstar, then re-validate parent; (ii) restrict parent hypothesis D=B(K). Pending: verify Kitaev th_almost_idemp generality (B(H) vs general C*). ALSO 2 minor: parent N1.6 JB3 should state j order-reflection explicitly; GT-cstar external citation typo 'Kitaev'->'Kitaev's' (byte-drift, non-load-bearing).
notes: GENERICITY VERDICT (careful proof-read of Kitaev): (ii)/(iii) — th_almost_idemp NOT a verbatim citation for D=⊕B(L_j), m>=2. C*-generic core CONFIRMED (easy part :2208-2237, Kadison-Schwarz :1691 general-C*-domain, assoc-cancellation algebra :2657/:2697-2719 — no factor/trace/center/type-I usage). The ONE B(H)-specific step: the canonical Stinespring STACK (:2587-2600) that proves the two-hole estimate Phi_assoc1/2, instantiated by Kitaev only over full B(H_n) (and the Choi rep :1568 needs full-B(K) domain). The single-step Stinespring rep (:1621) IS general-C*-domain, but the ITERATED TOWER over a proper-subalgebra codomain is ABSENT from refs. => Widening lem-cstar requires a NEW substantive bridge lemma (lem-kitaev-almostidemp-cstar: th_almost_idemp for UCP on finite-dim C* D), grounded on Stinespring :1621 + Kadison-Schwarz :1691 + re-instantiated stack :2587-2724 (codomain-iteration closes since Im Phi subset D). HOS 4.7.2 (:3228, C* sa is JB) handles GT-bhsa-jc. NUANCE: Stinespring dilations are onto FULL B(K), so D=B(K) is arguably the MORE NATURAL dilation space — restricting to it (option B) makes lem-cstar apply verbatim, cheap, and may not narrow the theorem's intent. DECISION PENDING USER: build the bridge lemma (general ⊕Mn) vs restrict D=B(K) (natural+cheap).
close_reason: Resolved by restricting D=B(K) (lem-cstar applies verbatim); general-D widen backlogged as aipm-us3

title: Restructure thm-dilation-compatible: factor 2 sub-lemmas + 1 def, derive the scaffold
description: Adversarial review showed the parent tree is a scaffold (all nodes inference=assumption) with two unproven load-bearing steps. RESTRUCTURE per L3/derivation-doctrine: (1) NEW def-eps-cstar-algebra (extended eps-C*-algebra) byte-matched from Kitaev (th_almost_idemp defines it). (2) NEW lemma lem-intertwine-spectral-idempotent: if Fj=jPhi (F=jC,Phi=Cj) then theta(2F-1)j=jtheta(2Phi-1); grounds via Kitaev Taylor-calculus + poly induction; INCLUDES the almost-idempotence inheritance ||Phi^2-Phi||<=||F^2-F|| (proven above, j order-isometric+submult). (3) NEW lemma lem-cstar-sa-to-epsjb (THE HEART): self-adjoint part of an extended O(eta)-C*-algebra under symmetrized product is an O(eta)-eps-JB order-unit algebra -- JB4 at O(eta) from Kitaev O(eta)-associativity (Phi_assoc1/2 @ approximate_algebras.tex ~2197-2231, needs CP). (4) Fix parent shard defs/deps: remove unused def-decomposable-map + thm-bridge; declare real imports (lem-P-properties, lem-intertwine-spectral-idempotent, lem-cstar-sa-to-epsjb, def-spectral-idempotent, def-near-fixed-algebra, def-eps-jb-algebra, def-eps-cstar-algebra). (5) Re-prove parent (~5-6 nodes) importing the sub-lemmas. (6) Minor: prune redundant HOS 3.4.3 external; its locus was off-by-2 (2567 not 2569). All grounds in EXISTING refs (Kitaev+HOS) -- no external blocker. Conclusion O(eta) mechanism confirmed real (Kitaev O(eta)-associativity needs CP; bridge alone gives only sqrt(eta)).
notes: 3 sub-lemmas VALIDATED + committed (A+B consensus). Parent thm-dilation-compatible now READY (all 4 deps validated). REMAINING: re-prove the parent (the original 11-node scaffold at proofs/thm-dilation-compatible/ is stale -> rebuild importing the 3 validated sub-lemmas + lem-P-properties; ~5-6 clean nodes) -> verify -> flip thm-dilation-compatible af: validated -> close aipm-ynq/aipm-vp5.

title: af-validate thm-dilation-compatible (Tier-1; dep thm-bridge validated)
description: Next af workspace after the bridge. Contract: conditional Kitaev-strength O(eta) result via reduction to Kitaev's CP theorem for the UCP lift F=jC. Risks found during scoping: (a) prose-proof file agent-B/theory/decomposable-dilation-compatible-theorem.md is MISSING (proof only in report/sections/10-exponent.tex); (b) sole def dep def-decomposable-map is status=draft/byte-check-pending (see aipm-9ho); (c) must find Kitaev CP theorem byte-verbatim in refs/kitaev-2405.02434/approximate_algebras.tex. Follow HANDOFF Recipe B.
notes: CHECKPOINT (prover+verifier+independent recheck): The verifier (agent-A) raised a CRITICAL false-premise finding (claimed counterexample ||F^2-F||~0.229<1/4 but ||Phi^2-Phi||~0.258>1/4). REFUTED by independent recheck: 7,008,000 samples over ALL unital 0/1 order-isometric embeddings j:ell-inf_nin->ell-inf_nout and random row-stochastic C -> ZERO violations of ||Phi^2-Phi||<=||F^2-F|| (worst ratio 1.0000). ANALYTIC PROOF: Phi^2-Phi=(Phi-I)Cj, F^2-F=j(Phi-I)C; j order-isometric => ||jM||=||M||, ||j||_op=1; so ||Phi^2-Phi||=||(Phi-I)Cj||<=||(Phi-I)C||=||j(Phi-I)C||=||F^2-F||. => theorem SOUND, hypotheses SUFFICIENT, P=theta(2Phi-1) well-defined, NO contract amendment for that reason. Node 1.3.3 just needs this 1-line derived node (not an assumption). REAL remaining work below.
close_reason: thm-dilation-compatible af: validated (A+B consensus; D=B(K); O(eta) genuine). The whole dilation theorem is COMPLETE.

title: Harden check-refs: require verbatim quotes (close skip_noquote evasion) + whole-quote match; retrofit lem-P-properties externals
description: check-refs.py (aipm-6ao) approved but has documented blind spots (reviewer, 2026-06-06): (1) skip_noquote EVASION — an external citing a refs locus WITHOUT a double-quoted verbatim string is skipped (WARN, not checked); 8 such externals exist (7 in lem-P-properties: GT-funcalc, GT-bhsa-complete, GT-end-banach, GT-binomial-series, GT-positive-unital, GT-kitaev-spectral, GT-order-unit-norm). A prover could paraphrase without quotes to evade. (2) single-run FALSE-NEGATIVE — gate requires only ONE >=40-char run, so a fabrication anchored to a real long run passes. (3) MIN_RUN=40 narrow margin. FIX: require every refs-citing external to embed a verbatim quote (skip_noquote -> FAIL after a grace pass); consider whole-quote (not single-run) matching with a tuned per-segment tolerance; retrofit lem-P-properties' 7 quote-less externals to embed verbatim refs quotes + re-verify the pilot. Each is a red->green test. Depends on the immediate fabrication fix landing first.

Full enforcement gate is a separate (deferred) bead.

title: Blocker C13: anchored separated-circuit negative-mass lower bound

Agent-B session failed to file this bead (bd config issue in its clone).

title: __probe__
description: probe

title: Acquire classical-layer ground-truth sources into refs/ (matrix norms, convexity, stochastic matrices)
description: Research + acquire authoritative, BYTE-EXTRACTABLE primary sources for the elementary finite-dim facts the classical layer needs (absent from current refs/, which is Jordan/operator-algebra only): (A) induced l-infinity operator/matrix norm = max absolute row sum + submultiplicativity; (B) affine maps preserve convex combinations, probability simplex faces/extreme points, 1-Lipschitz affine bounds; (C) row-stochastic matrices map the simplex to itself, products stochastic. Prefer open-access/arXiv (downloadable, stable sha256) over paywalled texts. Binding step: fetch real file -> refs/<id>/ + manifest/SOURCES.md + checksums.sha256, then ground classical lemmas by byte-match (check-refs gate). Unblocks aipm-v6e, aipm-71l with first-class provenance (instead of deriving inline through Kitaev's general operator-norm def). NOTE: lem-leakage sharpness -> depend on existing registry result ex-hume, no external needed.
notes: PARTIAL: acquired baake-sumner-2007.11433 (Baake-Sumner, On equal-input and monotone Markov matrices, J.Appl.Probab.2022) into refs/ as byte-greppable .tex (arXiv e-print source, hash-verified f358c71c, fetch-reproducible via sources.lock.json). Idempotent Markov-matrix structure (min poly x(x-1), extremal idempotents E_1..E_d, equal-input idempotents) for the commutative case (op-classical/def-stochastic/thm-simplex). Manifest+test updated; check-all green.

title: Split lem-leakage contract: separate groundable bound from ungrounded Hume sharpness
description: Scouting lem-leakage (RED) and lem-classical-equiv (AMBER) found the classical layer needs elementary finite-dimensional facts that are NOT byte-present in refs/: ||.||_{inf->inf} submultiplicativity & = max-row-l1; row-stochastic <=> affine self-map of Delta_n; affine commutes with convex combinations; 1-Lipschitz affine bounds. The refs store is Jordan/operator-algebra only. Per CLAUDE.md L1/section 9 this is a STOP-and-ask. OPTIONS: (a) acquire a convex-analysis / matrix-analysis reference into refs/ (which one?); (b) document a convention that internal consensus-definition bodies + their pure-algebra consequences are acceptable af leaves (amend CLAUDE.md L1 + HANDOFF Recipe B); (c) restrict the classical layer's scope. Blocks aipm-v6e and aipm-71l. Also: lem-leakage contract conflates the leakage bound with a Hume-family sharpness claim -> split the contract + register the Hume sharpness as its own result.
notes: RESOLVED the grounding-policy concern WITHOUT user input (over-escalated originally). Per the validated-bridge precedent — and just demonstrated in lem-idempotence-inheritance — elementary finite-dim matrix-norm/convex facts are DERIVED inline down to a GROUNDED refs primitive: Kitaev's general operator-norm definition (approximate_algebras.tex:638-642, ||w||=sup_{||Z||<=1}||w(Z)||, which covers finite-dim ||.||_{inf->inf} too) + internal consensus defs (def-stochastic) + affineness-as-definition. L1 STOP-and-ask applies to genuine EXTERNAL THEOREMS with no refs home, NOT to derivations from grounded primitives. No new reference, no L1 amendment needed. RESIDUAL (the only real gap, and self-doable): lem-leakage's contract conflates the groundable leakage bound with a Hume-family SHARPNESS claim ('no O(eta) closure possible') that has no refs home and is not a registry result -> SPLIT the contract (keep the af-able bound; move sharpness to its own result needing the Hume family registered). aipm-v6e/aipm-71l proceed via inline-derivation grounding; verifiers adjudicate any genuinely-missing leaf.

notes: UNBLOCKED in approach: ground the matrix-norm algebra by inline derivation from Kitaev's general operator-norm def (approximate_algebras.tex:638-642) + def-stochastic, same standard as the validated bridge / lem-idempotence-inheritance. No user decision needed (see aipm-9mw resolution). Still sequenced after the dilation sub-lemmas.

notes: PROOF PATTERN (square-hole verify, 2026-06-06): do NOT assert ||Phi||=1 / GT-phi-contraction as a leaf — it is a derivation AND it is AVOIDABLE. The bridge proofs only need ||Phi(a)|| <= (1+delta)||a|| (triangle: ||Phi(a)||<=||Phi(a)-a||+||a||, with ||Phi(a)-a||=||(Phi-P)(a)||<=delta||a|| from GT-Pprops since a=P(a)) and the reverse ||Phi(a)||>=(1-delta)||a||. So NO lem-positive-unital-contraction lemma is needed for the bridge (aipm-qpa stays optional cleanup). Instruct every bridge-build prover: ground via delta-bound + norm triangle, never ||Phi||=1.

title: af PR: post-hoc dependency-edge command (af depend <node> --on <ids>)
description: af (../vibefeld, /home/tobias/Projects/vibefeld) has NO command to add a dependency edge to an EXISTING node — --depends is settable only at 'af refine' creation time. Confirmed via af --help (only archive/deps/critical-path/update-external relate). This bites whenever a node added during a resolve round references an uncle/cousin node: the dep can't be declared post-hoc, forcing archive+recreate or re-grounding from a workspace-level external. Surfaced during the lem-bridge-orderunit pilot (node 1.2.1 used uncle node 1.1). User authorized af PRs (2026-06-06). Fix: add 'af depend <node-id> --on <id1,id2> -o <owner> -d <dir>' (and/or 'af refine --depends' parity) appending a dependency event to the append-only ledger; add a test; build; open PR on tobiasosborne/vibefeld. Non-blocking: re-grounding leaves from in-scope externals sidesteps it case-by-case.

title: [optional/backlog] Widen lem-cstar to general finite-dim C* (Stinespring-stack bridge lemma) -> general-D dilation theorem
description: thm-dilation-compatible is validated for D=B(K) (full matrix algebra, the natural Stinespring dilation target). To recover the general finite-dim C*-algebra D=⊕M_n version, build a bridge lemma lem-kitaev-almostidemp-cstar: Kitaev's th_almost_idemp for a UCP eta-idempotent map on a finite-dim C*-algebra D, grounded on the general-C*-domain Stinespring rep (kitaev approximate_algebras.tex:1621-1633) + Kadison-Schwarz (:1691-1694) + re-instantiating Kitaev's Stinespring stack (:2587-2724) over D (codomain-iteration closes since Im Phi subset D). Then widen lem-cstar-sa-to-epsjb 'B(H)'->'finite-dim C*' (re-ground GT-bhsa-jc via HOS 4.7.2 joa-m.md:3228) + re-prove parent. C*-generic core already confirmed (aipm-q8i). Marginal extra generality; deferred.

value: AF-PR AUTHORIZATION (user, 2026-06-06): authorized to proactively fix af (Adversarial Proof Framework) frictions/bugs and submit PRs upstream. af source = ../vibefeld (/home/tobias/Projects/vibefeld), v0.1.3. When an af friction/bug shows up during proof work, fix it in ../vibefeld + test + PR rather than silently working around it. Known candidate: af has no post-hoc dependency-edge command (deps recorded only in node text).

DERIVATION DOCTRINE (user, 2026-06-06): 'a derivation = lemma = af'. Pairs with the grounding rule. The proof DAG bottoms out ONLY at refs/ string-matches (grounded leaves = af externals). EVERYTHING above a leaf is a derivation, and a derivation IS a lemma = an af-validated claim (an af node; if reusable/substantial, its own registry lemma + tiny af workspace). No asserted 'elementary'/'standard' steps. E.g. ||y^2-z^2||<=2max(||y||,||z||)||y-z|| is NOT a leaf and NOT a handwave -> af-derive it from grounded operator-norm submultiplicativity, and factor it as its own reusable lemma (lem-sq-diff-bound).

FABRICATION INCIDENT + LESSON (2026-06-06): an af BUILD prover can write a plausible 'VERBATIM:' external quote that PARAPHRASES a true fact but does NOT byte-match the cited refs locus (GT-bhsa-jc fabricated in square-hole+bridge-polar). VERIFIERS MUST grep -F the FULL quoted string (not a substring) — square-hole's verifiers grep-confirmed a substring and missed the fabrication; bridge-polar's caught it. ENFORCEMENT: scripts/check-refs.py (aipm-6ao) byte-matches every external's verbatim quote vs refs/, wired into check-all.sh so the pre-commit hook blocks fabrications. Also: when WRITING build briefs, do NOT hand provers paraphrased 'verbatim' strings — they may copy them; instruct provers to COPY the actual refs bytes via grep and confirm the FULL string.

GROUNDING RULE (user, 2026-06-05): no 'standard facts'/'citations' — the ONLY ground truth is a byte/string match to a LOCAL refs/ source. Every result is a grounded leaf (statement string-matches a refs/ locus+sha256) OR proved via af down to grounded leaves. Consequence: results tagged 'cited' (kadison, effros-stormer, jnw, whitehead, aut-compact, vlw) must each be re-audited for a local string-match; PDF-only ones (whitehead, aut-compact) are ungrounded -> downgrade honestly. Linker bug: argument.py:133 requires ALL deps af:validated, blocking grounded-leaf deps (prop-kadison-js blocks the whole bridge after first-insertion/orderunit); design plan line 117 intended only 'internal-lemma deps' to require validation. Fix: grounded leaves count as available + add a refs-string-match gate enforcing it.

PROCESS (user, 2026-06-06): each discovered failure mode -> a RED->GREEN regression test (and a mechanical gate in check-all.sh where possible). Discovering failures via adversarial verifier passes is SUCCESS; the user expects more. Keep fresh-verifier passes rigorous (do not soften). Sharpens CLAUDE.md Rule 1. Ledger so far: fabricated verbatim external quote -> scripts/check-refs.py + test_check_refs.py (aipm-6ao); linker grounded-leaf block -> test_argument.py (50305fc).
